title: Million Dollar Maybe

jokes.text: Can I just ask, who the hell says po-tah-to?
jokes.setup: Family practicing the classic pronunciation song
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical question deflates the romantic premise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - logical, practical question that punctures pretension. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: our toast will do just what it's supposed to: Steal focus from the bride.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about performing at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals his selfish motivation disguised as reassurance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness revealed through his attempt to be supportive

jokes.text: Why is everyone staring at static?
jokes.setup: Family gathered around TV showing static
jokes.punchline: Bart doesn't understand why anyone would watch static
jokes.explanation: Simple generational gap humor about digital TV transition

jokes.text: I miss the history channel, damn it! How else would I know what I lived through?
jokes.setup: Digital TV conversion has knocked out their channels
jokes.punchline: Grandpa needs TV to remember his own lived experiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa joke - aging memory issues combined with TV dependency. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Here's all the money we would've given to televangelists.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to buy a TV converter
jokes.punchline: Marge casually reveals they've been giving money to televangelists
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the family's financial priorities, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Sir, your cheeks are ruddy! Did you eat a sugar cube when I wasn't watching?
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns playing golf simulation and appearing happy
jokes.punchline: Smithers' concern that Burns ate sugar without permission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns/Smithers dynamic - treats basic human happiness as medical emergency. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Wait, I'm shooting at nazis? That's not how I remember it.
jokes.setup: Grandpa playing a WWII video game
jokes.punchline: Suggests he remembers fighting alongside the Nazis
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor typical of Grandpa's confused wartime memories

jokes.text: She's not my girlfriend. She's a girl who I married, who used to be my friend.
jokes.setup: Bart teasing Homer about having a girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Homer's depressing but accurate description of marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer marriage humor - both sweet and depressing in typical fashion

jokes.text: Wow! That's exactly what I'm enjoying right now! Spooky.
jokes.setup: Homer reads fortune cookie saying he'll enjoy company of others
jokes.punchline: Homer is amazed the obvious prediction came true
jokes.explanation: Homer's gullibility about vague fortune cookie wisdom

jokes.text: My faith in the lord. It came back!
jokes.setup: Ned's fortune says something lost will return
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had lost his faith temporarily
jokes.explanation: Good Ned character moment revealing even he has moments of doubt

jokes.text: according to the only honest newspaper: The Beijing daily worker.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining fortune cookies are legitimate
jokes.punchline: Claims Chinese communist newspaper as only honest source
jokes.explanation: Political satire about media trust and communist propaganda

jokes.text: Hey, any part of a cookie you can't eat is just a waste of time.
jokes.setup: Discussion about fortune cookie validity
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical food-focused wisdom
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer philosophy - reduces complex issues to food concerns

jokes.text: Please be compact. Please be compact!
jokes.setup: Homer finds a disc, hopes for good luck
jokes.punchline: His specific hope it's a compact disc reveals his low standards
jokes.explanation: Homer's modest expectations and excitement over small things

jokes.text: I is!
jokes.setup: CD says 'He was' (presumably about being a lucky man)
jokes.punchline: Homer's grammatically incorrect but enthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Homer's poor grammar combined with his excitement

jokes.text: Maybe it'll speed up if I make 'oh, can you believe this' noises.
jokes.setup: Homer frustrated waiting in lottery line
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on complaining behavior
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about social behavior in lines, well-observed and self-aware

jokes.text: because my vows, apparently, weren't good enough.
jokes.setup: Couple will deliver their own vows
jokes.punchline: Officiant's bitter aside about his rejected vows
jokes.explanation: Good side character moment showing professional hurt feelings

jokes.text: like Mad Men, season two! I give it a year.
jokes.setup: Groom lists what he and bride have in common
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately predicts divorce based on shallow connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa cynicism and pop culture satire about modern relationships

jokes.text: He's strangling me! I'm gonna choke!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get on train without ticket
jokes.punchline: Conductor's dramatic overreaction to being grabbed
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy escalation, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: No time to pretend we're friends! Lotto me!
jokes.setup: Apu greeting Homer politely at Kwik-E-Mart
jokes.punchline: Homer's urgent dismissal of pleasantries with made-up verb
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer urgency with great made-up verb 'lotto me' - highly quotable and economical

jokes.text: Someone going that fast has no time for a ticket.
jokes.setup: Homer speeding past police
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's backwards logic about speed and urgency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - completely backwards but delivered with authority

jokes.text: Who's there? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Guest tries to start knock-knock joke at wedding
jokes.punchline: Immediate confusion and breakdown of basic joke format
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about failed attempt at crowd participation

jokes.text: Don't drag your kid into this!
jokes.setup: Knock-knock joke continues to fail badly
jokes.punchline: Guest's accusation escalates the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Continuation of cringe sequence with good escalation

jokes.text: Soon you will be mi...
jokes.setup: Sideshow Bob making his dramatic entrance to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-threat by stepping on rakes
jokes.explanation: Classic Sideshow Bob callback with perfect character integration and timing

jokes.text: A million dollars! Now I'm really glad I didn't die!
jokes.setup: Homer learns he won the lottery after his accident
jokes.punchline: Retroactively appreciates not dying for financial reasons
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's priorities and his relationship with mortality

jokes.text: Oh, I'm rich and I can't even tell my own wife!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he'd have to admit he missed the wedding toast
jokes.punchline: The burden of wealth becomes keeping it secret
jokes.explanation: Good setup for episode's central conflict and Homer's character flaw

jokes.text: That's right! I bought the winning ticket! And now I can buy back all the blood I sold!
jokes.setup: Barney claiming to be the lottery winner for Homer
jokes.punchline: His priority is buying back his own blood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - uniquely dark and specific to his character's desperation

jokes.text: This money will go to partially cover the cost of a study to decide what to do with the money.
jokes.setup: Government taking the lottery winnings for taxes
jokes.punchline: Circular bureaucratic logic about spending money on studying spending
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of government bureaucracy and wasteful spending studies

jokes.text: My advice is to buy hitler's baseball.
jokes.setup: Homer asks magic mirror for advice
jokes.punchline: Mirror specifically recommends Hitler's baseball as investment
jokes.explanation: Absurd dark humor about collectibles and historical artifacts

jokes.text: Here I am a millionaire, and I can't even help the woman who gave birth to me.
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional statement about helping family
jokes.punchline: Refers to Marge as 'the woman who gave birth to me' instead of his wife
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and Homer's confused family relationships

jokes.text: It's where I hide my adult magazines.
jokes.setup: Homer tells Barney to take anything from 'the money tree'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually where he hides pornography
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer misdirection about his hidden stash

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm big bill tilden!
jokes.setup: Grandpa playing Wii tennis
jokes.punchline: References obscure 1920s tennis champion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa reference to someone from his era that nobody else would know

jokes.text: I think I'm looking at a poop later.
jokes.setup: Grandpa exercising with Wii
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his bowel schedule
jokes.explanation: Typical elderly oversharing humor, moderately effective

jokes.text: You threw it, you go find it. Why, you little... There's a big box in here!
jokes.setup: Homer starting to strangle Bart
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by discovery of washing machine
jokes.explanation: Standard Simpsons violence interrupted by plot advancement

jokes.text: Not as much money as there used to be.
jokes.setup: Homer going to his tree stash for more money
jokes.punchline: Casually notes he's running low on his million dollars
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's careless spending habits

jokes.text: I should've been acting like rest-of-the-year Jerry Lewis.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes he's been too selfless with his money
jokes.punchline: Contrasts telethon Jerry Lewis with regular selfish Jerry Lewis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about Jerry Lewis's personality split between charity and regular life

jokes.text: I just go around saying that, in the hope it'll be applicable.
jokes.setup: Carl asks about Lenny's 'cut' of something
jokes.punchline: Lenny admits he just says this randomly hoping it will work
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about opportunistic behavior and hope-based strategies

jokes.text: I just go around saying that, in the hope it'll be applicable.
jokes.setup: Barney demanding his cut
jokes.punchline: He admits he just says this randomly hoping for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney logic and honesty about his opportunistic nature. Meta-commentary on greedy character tropes.

jokes.text: I said someone like me, I didn't say me.
jokes.setup: Band leader offers Homer a tambourine position
jokes.punchline: Homer clarifies he wasn't volunteering himself
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic and his habit of making suggestions he won't follow through on

jokes.text: I'm gonna call you benson and hedges 'cause you're gettin' smoked!
jokes.setup: Homer competing in zero gravity
jokes.punchline: Cigarette brand pun about defeating his opponent
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay typical of Homer's attempted trash talk

jokes.text: Tell me what my name is! Take away my roommate! He's dead!
jokes.setup: Elderly people making demands after being energized
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable requests to removing dead roommate
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nursing home conditions with good escalation

jokes.text: Blackmail your father in space, will you?!
jokes.setup: Bart threatening to tell Marge about the money
jokes.punchline: Homer's outrage at being blackmailed in zero gravity specifically
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity about the location of the blackmail attempt

jokes.text: You'd think it'd be dishwasher safe, but it wasn't.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why the video game is broken
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission he put electronics in dishwasher
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical approach to electronics and his matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Don't forget the low pay and the constant presence of death.
jokes.setup: Discussion about nursing home jobs being unpleasant
jokes.punchline: Casually adds death as a workplace hazard
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark workplace humor delivered with casual resignation

jokes.text: At least that's what the letter said. ENJAY! THE GUMMINT
jokes.setup: Marge thinks government sent them appliances as stimulus
jokes.punchline: Shows poorly forged letter with obvious spelling errors
jokes.explanation: Good sight gag and commentary on Marge's trusting nature

jokes.text: Evolve! Evolve! Get religious about a bird you see! Sprain your ankle and know it's a death sentence!
jokes.setup: Bart directing Homer's Neanderthal performance
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire commands about primitive human concerns
jokes.explanation: Clever anthropological humor with good escalation and specificity

jokes.text: Okay, but just 'cause I'm your best friend, don't be afraid to use that whip.
jokes.setup: Milhouse agreeing to pull Bart's rickshaw
jokes.punchline: Encourages his own mistreatment in the name of friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse pathetic eagerness to please, even when being exploited

jokes.text: We've got a million dollars?! Screw the toast!
jokes.setup: Homer confessing about missing the wedding toast for lottery
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately forgives everything when she hears about the money
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of Marge's priorities. Shows even the most moral character has a price.

jokes.text: I didn't know you could rent a balloon.
jokes.setup: Homer confessing about spending lottery money on experiences
jokes.punchline: Marge focuses on practical business detail instead of the deception
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment showing her practical priorities

tmdb_overview: Homer blows off Marge to buy a lottery ticket and winds up winning the million-dollar jackpot. Fearing how Marge will react if she finds out the reason Homer missed their date, Homer keeps his newly inherited fortune a secret and spoils his family with “anonymous” gifts. When Bart finds out, he and Homer spend the money like it’s going out of style, including buying front-row to tickets to see Coldplay in concert.
